message:
fix: Improve corrupted JSON handling test in throttling tests (bd-1jt.5.22)

- Fixed edge case test for corrupted triggered.json handling
- jq returns exit code 5 (not 1) for parse errors on some systems
- Updated test to accept any non-zero status as "not triggered"
- Fixed backoff function variable scoping with default values

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.2","title":"Write E2E tests for installer scripts using Docker","description":"# E2E Tests for Installer Scripts

## Purpose
Validate that generated install.sh scripts work correctly in real environments using Docker containers.

## Test File
`tests/e2e/test_installers.bats`

## Test Environments
- ubuntu:22.04 (Linux x64)
- debian:bookworm (alt Linux)
- arm64v8/ubuntu:22.04 (Linux ARM64, QEMU)

## Test Cases
- Basic install (binary download)
- Source build path (`--source`)
- Stale release detection
- Checksum verification
- Minisign verification (when available)
- Cache/offline mode (if enabled)
- Custom install prefix

## Logging
Each E2E test captures:
- Full Docker output
- Installer verbose logs
- Timing info

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tests pass on Ubuntu x64
- [ ] Source build tests work
- [ ] Signature + checksum verification tested
- [ ] Cache/offline mode tested
- [ ] Tests complete in 30 minutes or less","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:13:18.607161544Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T23:38:51.122576639Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T23:38:51.122545961Z","close_reason":"E2E Docker tests created in tests/e2e/test_installers_docker.sh. 12 tests (11 pass, 1 skip for ARM64).  {"id":"bd-1jt.5.20","title":"Tests for secrets/credential resolution + redaction","description":"# Tests for Secrets/Credential Resolution + Redaction

## Purpose
Ensure credential precedence is correct and sensitive values are never leaked to logs or JSON output.

## Coverage
- Precedence: env > config > gh auth token
- Missing token produces actionable error (non-interactive safe)
- Scope validation (contents:write, workflow)
- Redaction in `config show --json` and log output

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Redaction verified in JSON outputs
- [ ] Scope validation paths tested (mocked)
- [ ] Logs captured on failure","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:50:37.420544857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T23:27:57.535980599Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T23:27:57.535962836Z","close_reason":"Coverage complete: test_secrets.sh (19 tests) covers precedence, redaction (GitHub/Slack/Discord), JSON masking, webhook validation, and doctor summary. test_doctor.sh provides integration coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.20","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:50:37.420544857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.20","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:50:46.260840527Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.20","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:50:41.084322821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.21","title":"Tests for repos registry completeness + validation","description":"# Tests for Repos Registry Completeness + Validation

## Purpose
Ensure `repos.d/` registry is complete, valid, and safe to use for builds/releases/installers.

## Coverage
- Required fields per tool (repo, local_path, build_cmd, targets, workflow)
- `repos validate` reports missing fields
- Default `repos.d/` contains all 16 tools
- Discovery is read-only unless `--apply`
- Path normalization (absolute paths only)
- No duplicate tool names across files

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit tests for validation rules
- [ ] Integration tests for `dsr repos validate/list/info`
- [ ] Default `repos.d` completeness verified
- [ ] Logs captured on failure","notes":"Added scripts/tests/test_repos_validate.sh covering repos validate GoReleaser checks (targets, archive formats, name template) + missing repo/local_path error; shellcheck clean.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:50:52.825000561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T23:00:42.490843898Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T23:00:42.490493287Z","close_reason":"Tests complete: test_repos_validate.sh has validation rules, e2e_repos.sh has integration tests for validate/list/info, repos.d/ has all 16 tool configs, harness captures logs on failure","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.21","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:50:56.476851052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.21","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:51:00.379621792Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.21","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:50:52.825000561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.21","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:51:04.399836118Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.5.22","title":"Tests for throttling logic + watch debounce/dedupe","description":"# Tests for Throttling Logic + Watch Debounce/Dedupe

## Purpose
Verify queue-time detection and watch-mode behavior (debounce, jitter, dedupe) are correct and deterministic.

## Coverage
- queued > threshold triggers throttled
- in_progress with missing/old run_started_at treated as throttled
- debounce prevents flapping (requires N consecutive samples)
- dedupe prevents repeated triggers for same run_id
- backoff on API errors

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit tests cover queue-time calculations (time freeze)
- [ ] Watch mode dedupe + debounce tested with mocked runs
- [ ] Backoff paths covered

 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.3","title":"Write integration tests for dsr commands","description":"# Integration Tests for dsr Commands

## Purpose
Test that dsr commands work correctly with real (mocked) GH API responses and tool interactions, while preserving stream separation and JSON validity.

## Test File
`tests/integration/test_commands.bats`

## Test Cases (minimum)

### dsr check
```bash
@test "dsr check detects queued run over threshold" {
  export GH_MOCK_RESPONSE='[{"status":"queued","createdAt":"2026-01-30T12:00:00Z"}]'
  run dsr check ntm
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "THROTTLED"
}
```

### dsr watch
```bash
@test "dsr watch --once returns valid JSON" {
  run dsr watch --once --json
  echo "$output" | jq .
}
```

### dsr repos
```bash
@test "dsr repos list shows all 16 tools" {
  run dsr repos list
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "ntm"
}
```

### dsr build (dry-run)
```bash
@test "dsr build --dry-run shows planned actions" {
  run dsr build ntm --dry-run
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Would build"
}
```

### dsr release (dry-run)
```bash
@test "dsr release --dry-run shows upload plan" {
  mkdir -p /tmp/test-artifacts
  touch /tmp/test-artifacts/ntm-linux-amd64
  run dsr release ntm v1.0.0 --artifacts /tmp/test-artifacts --dry-run
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Would upload"
}
```

### dsr status/report
```bash
@test "dsr status --json outputs schema" {
  run dsr status --json
  echo "$output" | jq .
}
```

## Mock Setup
- `tests/helpers/mock_gh.bash` for API responses
- `tests/helpers/mock_ssh.bash` for remote builds

## Logging Requirements
- All tests must use shared harness (log capture on failure)
- Ensure stdout-only JSON when `--json` (stderr empty unless error)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All dsr commands have integration coverage
- [ ] Mocking infrastructure works
- [ ] Tests run in under 2 minutes
- [ ] Edge cases covered (errors, timeouts)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:15:05.278042146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T20:15:31.974636721Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T20:15:31.974493411Z","close_reason":"Implemented doctor, dsr build/release (dry-run), JSON output validation, stream separation tests, exit code consistency, and mock infrastructure verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:15:56.036145566Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:52:19.595834242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:36:13.256330768Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:15:58.531434369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:36:10.308760448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:15:05.278042146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:53:22.191529810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:36:16.790152498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.4","title":"Implement comprehensive logging infrastructure for dsr","description":"# Comprehensive Logging Infrastructure for dsr

## Purpose
Provide deterministic, structured logs for every action so failures are diagnosable, retries are safe, and audit trails exist for releases.

## Core Principles
- **Structured JSON Lines** for machine parsing
- **stderr** for human logs (colored), **stdout** reserved for JSON output
- **No \`set -e\`**: explicit error handling with captured exit codes
- Stable \`DSR_RUN_ID\` to correlate events across commands
- **Verbosity levels**: error, warn, info, debug (configurable via -v/-q flags)

## File: src/logging.sh
\`\`\`bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
# logging.sh - Structured logging for dsr

set -uo pipefail

# Log levels (numeric for comparison)
declare -A LOG_LEVELS=(
  [error]=0
  [warn]=1
  [info]=2
  [debug]=3
)

# Current log level (default: info)
LOG_LEVEL="${DSR_LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
LOG_FILE="${DSR_LOG_FILE:-}"

# Colors (disabled if not TTY or NO_COLOR set)
if [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" && -t 2 ]]; then
  _RED=$'\033[0;31m' _YELLOW=$'\033[0;33m'
  _GREEN=$'\033[0;32m' _BLUE=$'\033[0;34m'
  _GRAY=$'\033[0;90m' _NC=$'\033[0m'
else
  _RED='' _YELLOW='' _GREEN='' _BLUE='' _GRAY='' _NC=''
fi

# Generate run ID if not set
: "${DSR_RUN_ID:="run-$(date +%s)-$$"}"

# Initialize logging
log_init() {
  local state_dir="${DSR_STATE_DIR:-$HOME/.local/state/dsr}"
  local log_dir="$state_dir/logs"
  mkdir -p "$log_dir"
  
  # Set log file if not specified
  if [[ -z "$LOG_FILE" ]]; then
    LOG_FILE="$log_dir/dsr-$(date +%Y%m%d).log"
  fi
  
  # Rotate logs older than 30 days
  find "$log_dir" -name 'dsr-*.log' -mtime +30 -delete 2>/dev/null || true
  
  # Compress logs older than 7 days
  find "$log_dir" -name 'dsr-*.log' -mtime +7 \! -name '*.gz' \
    -exec gzip -q {} \; 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Check if level should be logged
_should_log() {
  local level="$1"
  [[ "${LOG_LEVELS[$level]}" -le "${LOG_LEVELS[$LOG_LEVEL]}" ]]
}

# Core log function
_log() {
  local level="$1" msg="$2"
  shift 2
  local extras="$*"
  
  _should_log "$level" || return 0
  
  local ts
  ts="$(date -Iseconds)"
  
  # Build JSON log entry
  local json="{"
  json+="\"ts\":\"$ts\","
  json+="\"run_id\":\"$DSR_RUN_ID\","
  json+="\"level\":\"$level\","
  json+="\"cmd\":\"${DSR_CURRENT_CMD:-}\","
  json+="\"msg\":\"$msg\""
  
  # Add optional context fields
  [[ -n "${DSR_CURRENT_TOOL:-}" ]] && json+=",\"tool\":\"$DSR_CURRENT_TOOL\""
  [[ -n "${DSR_CURRENT_HOST:-}" ]] && json+=",\"host\":\"$DSR_CURRENT_HOST\""
  [[ -n "$extras" ]] && json+=",$extras"
  json+="}"
  
  # Write to log file
  [[ -n "$LOG_FILE" ]] && echo "$json" >> "$LOG_FILE"
  
  # Human-readable stderr output
  local color
  case "$level" in
    error) color="$_RED" ;;
    warn)  color="$_YELLOW" ;;
    info)  color="$_GREEN" ;;
    debug) color="$_GRAY" ;;
  esac
  
  echo "${color}[$level]${_NC} $msg" >&2
}

# Convenience functions
log_error() { _log error "$1" "${2:-}"; }
log_warn()  { _log warn "$1" "${2:-}"; }
log_info()  { _log info "$1" "${2:-}"; }
log_debug() { _log debug "$1" "${2:-}"; }

# Log with duration tracking
log_timed() {
  local start_ts=$(date +%s%3N)
  "$@"
  local exit_code=$?
  local end_ts=$(date +%s%3N)
  local duration_ms=$((end_ts - start_ts))
  
  log_info "Completed: $1" "\"duration_ms\":$duration_ms,\"exit_code\":$exit_code"
  return $exit_code
}

# Export functions
export -f log_init log_error log_warn log_info log_debug log_timed
export DSR_RUN_ID LOG_LEVEL LOG_FILE
\`\`\`

## Log Locations
- \`~/.local/state/dsr/logs/dsr-YYYYMMDD.log\` - Daily session logs
- \`~/.local/state/dsr/builds/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>/\` - Per-build logs
- \`~/.local/state/dsr/builds/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>/<host>.log\` - Per-host build logs

## Log Schema (JSONL)
\`\`\`json
{"ts":"2026-01-30T13:14:22Z","run_id":"run-1738246462-12345","level":"info","cmd":"build","tool":"ntm","msg":"Starting build","host":"trj"}
{"ts":"2026-01-30T13:14:25Z","run_id":"run-1738246462-12345","level":"info","cmd":"build","tool":"ntm","msg":"Completed: go build","duration_ms":3200,"exit_code":0}
{"ts":"2026-01-30T13:14:26Z","run_id":"run-1738246462-12345","level":"error","cmd":"build","tool":"ntm","msg":"Build failed","exit_code":1}
\`\`\`

## Required Fields
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| ts | ISO8601 | Yes | Timestamp |
| run_id | string | Yes | Correlation ID |
| level | enum | Yes | error/warn/info/debug |
| cmd | string | Yes | Current command |
| msg | string | Yes | Log message |
| tool | string | Contextual | Tool being processed |
| host | string | Contextual | Build host |
| duration_ms | int | Timed ops | Operation duration |
| exit_code | int | On completion | Exit code |

## Retention Policy
- **Keep**: Last 30 days of session logs
- **Compress**: Logs older than 7 days (gzip)
- **Delete**: Logs older than 30 days

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] log_init creates log directory and sets up file
- [ ] log_error/warn/info/debug respect LOG_LEVEL
- [ ] log_timed captures duration and exit code
- [ ] stderr gets colored human-readable output
- [ ] log file gets JSONL machine-readable output
- [ ] run_id correlates all logs from single invocation
- [ ] Retention policy auto-cleans old logs
- [ ] NO_COLOR disables colored output","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:17:36.274359993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:29:54.251846660Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T17:29:54.251827113Z","close_reason":"Implemented logging infrastructure in src/logging.sh. All 12 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:34:06.498228160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
